scenario: Process has versionTag = "v2.1.0" attribute. Team wonders if affects engine vs being documentary. Process versionTag attribute?
question: Process versionTag attribute?

options.text: The versionTag attribute in Camunda 8 directly affects process execution behavior and routing decisions at runtime -- this does not accurately reflect the behavior. The versionTag is a human-readable custom label for release tracking and documentation. It does not influence how the Zeebe engine executes process instances, routes tokens, or applies business logic. The engine auto-incremented version number is the authoritative version for execution routing. The versionTag serves as a binding lookup key for bindingType="versionTag" configurations but does not alter runtime execution behavior.

The versionTag attribute is a supported feature in Camunda 8 set via zeebe:versionTag on the <bpmn:process> element. It appears in process definition listings through Camunda Operate and APIs. The feature is available and serves both as a documentary release label and as a resource binding key for bindingType="versionTag" configurations.

versionTag is a documentary label AND a binding key: set via zeebe:versionTag on <bpmn:process>, it serves two purposes. 0") alongside the auto-incremented engine version. As a binding key it enables bindingType="versionTag" on Call Activities, Business Rule Tasks, and Linked Forms to resolve resource versions by tag rather than by deployment. This dual role makes it more than pure documentation -- it actively participates in resource version resolution.

Use case 3: Audit reference: "ticket-456": link to issue tracker. Use case 4: Environment marker: "v2.1.0-staging": vs "v2.1.0-prod". For visibility: Operate: may display versionTag (verify per version). API responses: include in process definition data. For querying: By versionTag (verify support). Common query: by engine version + versionTag combined. For deployment: Deployer sets versionTag: at modeling time. Or programmatically: at deployment API. For multi-cluster: Same processId: in dev / staging / prod. Different engine versions per cluster. Same versionTag: links across. For CI/CD: versionTag from git tag: automated. Traceability: engine version + git SHA. For audit: Which versionTag deployed when. Compliance: traceable. For backwards compatibility: Old versions retain versionTag. History preserved. For runtime behavior: Documentary by default: shows in Operate / API list. Lookup key for versionTag binding: Call Activity / Business Rule Task / Linked Form with bindingType="versionTag" + versionTag="vX.Y.Z" resolves to the latest deployed resource matching that tag (call-activities.md:25). For Call Activity binding (per Q10): Default latest + deployment bindings: use engine-version routing. bindingType="versionTag" binding: resolves directly by versionTag -- making the tag load-bearing for resource resolution, not just metadata.
